skill.name: context7
skill.description: Fetch up-to-date library documentation via Context7 API. Use PROACTIVELY when: (1) Working with ANY external library (React, Next.js, Supabase, etc.), (2) User asks about library APIs, patterns, or best practices, (3) Implementing features that rely on third-party packages, (4) Debugging library-specific issues, (5) Need current documentation beyond training data cutoff, (6) When installing dependencies or frameworks, check docs for latest versions.
skill.summary: Fetch up-to-date library documentation via Context7 API for any external library.
skill.author: am-will

security_audit.summary: All static findings are false positives. Environment variable access is legitimate API key usage for Context7 service. Network requests go only to context7.com API. External commands and filesystem references are documentation examples, not executable code. No malicious intent or security risks detected.

content.value_statement: Claude Code training data has cutoff dates. This skill fetches current library documentation from Context7 API so you always use accurate APIs and patterns.
actual_capabilities: Search for libraries by name via Context7 API
Fetch current documentation context for specific libraries
Retrieve library metadata including IDs and version info
Query documentation with natural language questions
Output results in text or markdown format
Limit response tokens for context management
limitations: Requires valid Context7 API key to function
Only works for libraries indexed by Context7 service
Network connectivity required for API requests
Documentation quality depends on Context7 index coverage
Cannot access documentation behind authentication walls

use_cases.title: Implement React Features with Current APIs
use_cases.description: Developer building a React component needs to verify the latest useEffect hook syntax and cleanup patterns. Use this skill to fetch current React documentation instead of relying on potentially outdated knowledge.

use_cases.title: Debug Next.js App Router Issues
use_cases.description: Developer encountering errors with Next.js App Router middleware needs current best practices. This skill retrieves the latest Next.js documentation to resolve version-specific issues.

use_cases.title: Integrate Supabase Authentication
use_cases.description: Developer adding Supabase row-level security to an application needs current API patterns. Fetch Supabase authentication documentation to implement correct security setup.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Library Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for documentation about the React library to understand its current APIs and patterns.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner users wanting to explore a library before using it

prompt_templates.title: Specific API Query
prompt_templates.prompt: Fetch documentation for the Next.js library about app router middleware setup and configuration.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate users implementing specific features with known libraries

prompt_templates.title: Version-Specific Patterns
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for Supabase documentation then query about row-level security implementation patterns for authentication.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced users needing detailed implementation guidance for complex features

prompt_templates.title: Dependency Installation Research
prompt_templates.prompt: Before installing the Next.js framework, search for its documentation to identify the latest version and any breaking changes from previous versions.
prompt_templates.scenario: Proactive users checking current versions before adding dependencies

output: Description: A JavaScript library for building user interfaces
Use this ID with the context command to fetch specific documentation
useEffect cleanup function syntax and patterns...
Current best practices for component unmounting...

best_practices: Always search for library documentation before implementing features to ensure current API usage
Use specific, targeted queries when fetching context to get relevant documentation sections
Verify library IDs from search results before making context requests
Set appropriate token limits when working with large documentation sets
anti_patterns: Do not guess library APIs based on training data - documentation may have changed
Do not skip the search step - library IDs change and direct guessing fails
Do not use vague queries like 'everything' - be specific about what you need
Do not rely on cached documentation - always fetch fresh context for current work

faq.question: What is Context7 and why do I need an API key?
faq.answer: Context7 is a documentation service that indexes current library documentation. The API key is required to authenticate requests to their service and is obtained from Context7 directly.

faq.question: Where do I store the Context7 API key?
faq.answer: Store the API key in a .env file in the skill installation folder. Common locations are ~/.codex/skills/context7/.env or ~/.claude/skills/context7/.env.

faq.question: Can I use this skill without internet access?
faq.answer: No. This skill requires network connectivity to make requests to the Context7 API at context7.com. Offline use is not supported.

faq.question: What libraries are supported by Context7?
faq.answer: Context7 indexes many popular libraries and frameworks. Search for your desired library first to confirm availability. If the library is not indexed, the search will return no results.

faq.question: Why does the skill require an API key instead of being free?
faq.answer: Context7 is a third-party service that hosts and indexes documentation. The API key enables authenticated access to their service and helps maintain service availability.

faq.question: How do I find the correct library ID for context requests?
faq.answer: First run the search command with your library name. The search results include an 'id' field. Use this exact ID in the context command to fetch documentation for that library.
